title: Life in the Wild Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Something that can cause harm or danger.
rounds.answer: threat
rounds.explanation: threat: Something that can cause harm or danger.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A ceremony to remember and say goodbye to someone who has died.
rounds.answer: funeral
rounds.explanation: funeral: A ceremony to remember and say goodbye to someone who has died.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Something that can be seen or noticed.
rounds.answer: observable
rounds.explanation: observable: Something that can be seen or noticed.

games.title: Spelling Builder

rounds.clue: A ceremony to remember and say goodbye to someone who has died.
rounds.answer: funeral

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The dark clouds are a threat of a coming storm.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The path through the mountains was treacherous after the rains.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The soldiers tried to prohibit gatherings in the village.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from 'The Red Cloth, part 1': People called him a _____, someone who gave orders no one could question.
rounds.answer: dictator
rounds.explanation: The text says: People called him a dictator, someone who gave orders no one could question.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from 'The Red Cloth, part 2': Write how they faced _____, and how they responded.” Nadia wrote about her grandmother, who had once hidden refugees fleeing...
rounds.answer: hardship
rounds.explanation: The text says: Write how they faced hardship, and how they responded.” Nadia wrote about her grandmother, who had once hidden refugees fleeing the war.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from 'Life in the Wild: source text 2': They _____ months of dry heat and burst into life when the rains finally come.
rounds.answer: endure
rounds.explanation: The text says: They endure months of dry heat and burst into life when the rains finally come.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3: Four-Minute Unit Talk

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5: Explain and Respond
rounds.target: Explain one important unit idea, give a reason with because, and respond to two follow-up questions.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The administration centre was a stone building filled with rules.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The sky turned crimson as the sun set behind the mountains.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The villagers chose peaceful resistance instead of violent actions.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'bureaucracy' mean in this unit?
choices: A system of managing a government or organisation with many rules and officials.
Something that can cause harm or danger.
A leader who has complete power and makes all the rules without asking others.
rounds.answer: A system of managing a government or organisation with many rules and officials.
rounds.explanation: bureaucracy: A system of managing a government or organisation with many rules and officials.

rounds.prompt: What does 'crimson' mean in this unit?
choices: Something that can cause harm or danger.
A leader who has complete power and makes all the rules without asking others.
rounds.answer: A deep red colour.
rounds.explanation: crimson: A deep red colour.

rounds.prompt: What does 'dictator' mean in this unit?
choices: A difficult situation that causes suffering or trouble.
A leader who has complete power and makes all the rules without asking others.
Something that can cause harm or danger.
rounds.answer: A leader who has complete power and makes all the rules without asking others.
rounds.explanation: dictator: A leader who has complete power and makes all the rules without asking others.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'starvation'?
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: starvation is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'violent'?
rounds.answer: Adjective
rounds.explanation: violent is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'endure'?
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: endure is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A leader who has complete power and makes all the rules without asking others.
To treat someone badly or unfairly because of their beliefs or identity.
To say something loudly or officially.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: To say no to something or not accept it.
A person who wants to end something, especially slavery.
A serious illness caused by bacteria that can spread through dirty water or food.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: A large group of people gathered together.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: What does 'oppress' mean?
choices: To keep going through something difficult without giving up.
To say that something is not allowed.
To treat someone badly or unfairly because of their beliefs or identity.
To treat someone in a cruel or unfair way, making them feel weak or powerless.
rounds.answer: To treat someone in a cruel or unfair way, making them feel weak or powerless.
rounds.explanation: The soldiers tried to oppress the villagers by taking away their rights.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from 'Life in the Wild: source text 2': When plastic waste reaches the ocean, it becomes a _____ to sea turtles who mistake it for jellyfish.
rounds.answer: threat
rounds.explanation: The text says: When plastic waste reaches the ocean, it becomes a threat to sea turtles who mistake it for jellyfish.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from 'Life in the Wild: source text 2': The balance of nature is _____ — it can look strong, but it is easily broken.
rounds.answer: treacherous
rounds.explanation: The text says: The balance of nature is treacherous — it can look strong, but it is easily broken.
